# MindCheck Tools

> Free, private mental health self-checks. Your answers are scored in your browser and never stored.

MindCheck Tools provides validated, evidence-based mental health screening instruments as free, private web tools. All scoring happens client-side — no accounts, no login, no data collection.

## Self-Check Tools

### PHQ-9 Depression Self-Check
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/phq-9-depression-test
- Description: 9-question validated depression screener (Patient Health Questionnaire-9). Scores 0-27 across minimal, mild, moderate, moderately severe, and severe ranges.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Source: Developed by Drs. Spitzer, Williams, and Kroenke. Public domain.
- Questions answered: Am I depressed? How severe are my depression symptoms? What does my PHQ-9 score mean?

### CES-D Depression Scale
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/ces-d-depression-scale
- Description: 20-item self-report depression screening tool developed by NIMH (Radloff, 1977). Items rated 0-3 for past week. Total score 0-60. Items 4, 8, 12, 16 are reverse-scored positive-affect items. Clinical cutoff of 16+ suggests further evaluation for depression.
- Time: ~5 minutes
- Source: Developed by Lenore Radloff at the National Institute of Mental Health. Public domain (U.S. government work).
- Questions answered: What is the CES-D? CES-D depression test online. CES-D scoring. CES-D vs PHQ-9. What does a CES-D score of 16 mean?

### GAD-7 Anxiety Self-Check
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/gad-7-anxiety-test
- Description: 7-question validated generalized anxiety screener. Scores 0-21 across minimal, mild, moderate, and severe ranges.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Source: Developed by Drs. Spitzer, Kroenke, Williams, and Löwe. Public domain.
- Questions answered: Do I have anxiety? How bad is my anxiety? What does my GAD-7 score mean?

### AUDIT Alcohol Use Screen
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/audit-alcohol-test
- Description: 10-item WHO alcohol screening tool to reflect on relationship with alcohol. Scores 0-40 across low-risk, hazardous, harmful, and possible dependence ranges.
- Time: ~3 minutes
- Source: World Health Organization. Public domain.
- Questions answered: Do I drink too much? Is my drinking a problem? What is AUDIT alcohol screening?

### AUDIT-C Quick Screen
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/audit-c-alcohol-screen
- Description: 3-question brief alcohol screen used in primary care settings worldwide.
- Time: ~1 minute
- Source: Derived from WHO AUDIT. Public domain.
- Questions answered: Quick alcohol screening, brief alcohol use assessment.

### DAST-10 Drug Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/dast-10-drug-screening
- Description: 10-item validated drug abuse screening test. Scores 0-10 across no problems, low, moderate, substantial, and severe ranges.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Source: Developed by Dr. Harvey Skinner. Public domain.
- Questions answered: Do I have a drug problem? Drug abuse screening test. What does my DAST-10 score mean?

### CAGE-AID Substance Use Screen
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/cage-aid-substance-abuse-screening
- Description: 4-item validated screening tool for both alcohol and drug use concerns. Adapted from the original CAGE to include drugs. Score of 2+ is a positive screen.
- Time: ~1 minute
- Source: Adapted from the CAGE questionnaire by Dr. John Ewing. Public domain.
- Questions answered: Do I have a substance abuse problem? CAGE-AID screening test. What does a positive CAGE-AID screen mean?

### PCL-5 PTSD Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/pcl-5-ptsd-screening
- Description: 20-item validated PTSD screening measure (PTSD Checklist for DSM-5). Scores 0-80 across four DSM-5 symptom clusters. Cutoff score of 33 suggests probable PTSD.
- Time: ~5 minutes
- Source: Developed by the National Center for PTSD, U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs. Public domain.
- Questions answered: Do I have PTSD? What does my PCL-5 score mean? What is the PCL-5 cutoff score?

### PC-PTSD-5 PTSD Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/pc-ptsd-5-screening
- Description: 5-item yes/no PTSD screen developed by the VA National Center for PTSD (Primary Care PTSD Screen for DSM-5). Begins with a trauma exposure gate question, then asks about five past-month symptoms: nightmares/intrusive memories, avoidance, hypervigilance, numbness/detachment, and guilt/blame. Score 0-5, cutoff of 3+ is a positive screen.
- Time: ~1 minute
- Source: Developed by the National Center for PTSD, U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs. Validated by Prins et al. (2016). Public domain.
- Questions answered: Do I have PTSD? Quick PTSD screening test. What is the PC-PTSD-5? VA PTSD screening tool. PC-PTSD-5 vs PCL-5.

### ASRS ADHD Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/asrs-adhd-screening
- Description: 6-item WHO-developed adult ADHD screening tool (Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale v1.1 Part A). Uses research-validated thresholds where different questions have different positive cutoffs. 4+ positive responses suggests further evaluation is warranted.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Source: Developed by the World Health Organization (WHO) in collaboration with leading ADHD researchers. Public domain.
- Questions answered: Do I have ADHD? Adult ADHD screening test. What does the ASRS score mean?

### MDQ Bipolar Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/mdq-bipolar-screening
- Description: Validated 3-part Mood Disorder Questionnaire (MDQ) for bipolar spectrum disorders. 13 symptom questions, 1 co-occurrence question, 1 functional impact question. Positive screen requires all 3 criteria met (7+ symptoms, co-occurrence, moderate+ impact).
- Time: ~3 minutes
- Source: Developed by Robert M.A. Hirschfeld, MD, and colleagues. Free to reproduce for clinical and research purposes.
- Questions answered: Do I have bipolar disorder? Bipolar screening test. What does a positive MDQ screen mean?

### OCI-R OCD Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/oci-r-ocd-screening
- Description: 18-item validated OCD screening tool (Obsessive-Compulsive Inventory - Revised). Scores 0-72 with clinical cutoff at 21. Includes 6 subscales: Hoarding, Checking, Ordering, Neutralizing, Washing, Obsessing.
- Time: ~3 minutes
- Source: Developed by Foa et al. (2002). Free to use in clinical and research settings.
- Questions answered: Do I have OCD? OCD screening test. What does my OCI-R score mean? What are OCD subtypes?

### DASS-21 Depression, Anxiety & Stress Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/dass-21-depression-anxiety-stress
- Description: 21-item validated screening tool that measures depression, anxiety, and stress simultaneously. Three separate subscale scores (0-42 each) with 5 severity levels per subscale. Scores are raw totals multiplied by 2 (DASS-21 short form equivalent).
- Time: ~4 minutes
- Source: Developed by Lovibond & Lovibond (1995). Public domain.
- Questions answered: Am I depressed, anxious, or stressed? DASS-21 screening test. What does my DASS-21 score mean? Depression vs anxiety vs stress.

### WHO-5 Well-Being Index
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/who-5-wellbeing-index
- Description: 5-item positive wellbeing measure from the World Health Organization. Each item rated 0-5 (At no time to All of the time) for the past 2 weeks. Raw score 0-25, multiplied by 4 for percentage score 0-100%. Score below 50% suggests further evaluation; below 28% suggests depression screening with PHQ-9.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Source: Developed by the World Health Organization (1998). Public domain.
- Questions answered: What is the WHO-5? How is the WHO-5 scored? WHO-5 well-being index test. Mental wellbeing assessment. Am I doing okay mentally?

### K6 Psychological Distress Scale
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/k6-distress-scale
- Description: 6-item Kessler Psychological Distress Scale measuring nonspecific psychological distress over the past 30 days. Items rated 0 (None of the time) to 4 (All of the time). Total score 0-24. Score of 13+ = serious psychological distress (SPD), the same threshold used in SAMHSA national surveys.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Source: Developed by Ronald Kessler et al. at Harvard Medical School in collaboration with the WHO. Public domain.
- Questions answered: What is the K6? K6 distress scale test. Psychological distress screening. What does a K6 score of 13 mean? K6 vs K10.

### SCOFF Eating Disorder Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/scoff-eating-disorder-screening
- Description: 5-item validated eating disorder screening tool (SCOFF questionnaire). Yes/No format screening for anorexia, bulimia, binge eating disorder, and other eating disorders. Score of 2+ is a positive screen. Sensitivity ~84.6%, specificity ~89.6%.
- Time: ~1 minute
- Source: Developed by Morgan et al. (1999). Free to use in clinical and research settings.
- Questions answered: Do I have an eating disorder? Eating disorder screening test. What does SCOFF stand for? What does a positive SCOFF screen mean?

### AQ-10 Autism Spectrum Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/aq-10-autism-screening
- Description: 10-item validated autism spectrum screening tool (Autism Spectrum Quotient - 10 item). 4-option response scale with binary scoring. Score of 6+ is a positive screen per NICE guidelines. Screens for social communication, attention switching, detail orientation, and imagination traits.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Source: Developed by Simon Baron-Cohen et al. at the Autism Research Centre, University of Cambridge. Free to use.
- Questions answered: Do I have autism? Adult autism screening test. What does my AQ-10 score mean? What is the AQ-10 cutoff score?

### Sobriety Calculator & Day Counter
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/sobriety-calculator
- Description: Free sobriety day counter. Enter your clean date, track days sober, see recovery milestones (24h to 10 years), next milestone countdown, motivational messages, and estimated money saved. Date saves in browser localStorage.
- Time: ~1 minute
- Questions answered: How many days sober am I? Sobriety milestone tracker. Clean date calculator. Recovery day counter.

### Money Saved in Recovery Calculator
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/money-saved-recovery-calculator
- Description: Calculate money saved by not using alcohol, cigarettes, cannabis, cocaine, opioids, or methamphetamine. Preset spending averages by substance, fun comparisons, and 1/5/10 year savings projections.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Questions answered: How much money saved not drinking? Cost of addiction calculator. Savings from quitting smoking. Financial impact of recovery.

### Health Recovery Timeline
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/health-recovery-timeline
- Description: Interactive visual timeline showing what happens to your body after you quit alcohol, cigarettes/nicotine, or opioids. Based on medical research. Shows past milestones as completed and future ones with countdowns.
- Time: ~1 minute
- Questions answered: What happens when you stop drinking timeline? Body recovery after quitting alcohol. Quit smoking health timeline. Opioid recovery timeline.

### BAC Calculator
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/bac-calculator
- Description: Blood alcohol content estimator using the Widmark formula. Inputs: biological sex, weight, number of drinks, time elapsed, drink type. Outputs: estimated BAC, legal status, effects at current level, time until sober, visual BAC gauge.
- Time: ~1 minute
- Questions answered: What is my BAC? How drunk am I? When will I be sober? Blood alcohol calculator. How long does alcohol stay in system?

### Standard Drinks Calculator
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/standard-drinks-calculator
- Description: Calculate how many US standard drinks (14g pure alcohol) are in any beverage. Enter container size and alcohol percentage, or browse 16 presets for beer, wine, spirits, and cocktails. Visual comparison bars show that many common drinks contain more than one standard drink.
- Time: ~1 minute
- Questions answered: How many standard drinks is that? What is a standard drink? Standard drink calculator. How many drinks in a bottle of wine? How many standard drinks in a pint of beer?

### HALT Check-In
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/halt-check-in
- Description: Daily recovery check-in based on the HALT acronym (Hungry, Angry, Lonely, Tired). Rate each dimension on a 1-5 scale and see your current vulnerability level with personalized coping suggestions. Used widely in 12-step and recovery programs.
- Time: ~1 minute
- Questions answered: Am I at risk for relapse? HALT recovery tool. HALT check in. Daily recovery self-check. What does HALT stand for in recovery?

### Withdrawal Timeline
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/withdrawal-timeline
- Description: Informational tool showing detailed withdrawal timelines for 8 substances: alcohol, opioids (heroin/fentanyl), opioids (prescription), benzodiazepines, cocaine/stimulants, methamphetamine, cannabis, and nicotine. Phase-by-phase symptoms with severity ratings, PAWS information, MAT options, and critical safety warnings for life-threatening withdrawal types.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Questions answered: How long does withdrawal last? Alcohol withdrawal timeline. Opioid withdrawal symptoms day by day. Benzo withdrawal timeline. Drug withdrawal symptoms. What is medical detox?

### Treatment Cost Estimator
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/treatment-cost-estimator
- Description: Informational tool showing estimated cost ranges for addiction treatment types: outpatient counseling, IOP, PHP, residential rehab (30/60/90 days), medical detox, MAT (Suboxone, methadone, Vivitrol), and sober living. Includes insurance coverage info (Mental Health Parity Act), financial assistance options, and SAMHSA Treatment Locator.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Questions answered: How much does rehab cost? Drug treatment cost. Addiction treatment cost estimator. Does insurance cover rehab? How to pay for rehab.

### Relapse Prevention Plan Builder
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/relapse-prevention-plan
- Description: Free interactive worksheet for building a personalized relapse prevention plan. 7-section builder covering triggers, warning signs, coping strategies, emergency contacts, safe activities, places to avoid, and a craving action plan. Generates a printable plan document.
- Time: ~10 minutes
- Questions answered: How do I make a relapse prevention plan? Relapse prevention worksheet. Trigger identification. Craving action plan. Recovery planning tool.

### Urge Surfing Timer
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/urge-surfing-timer
- Description: Guided mindfulness timer for riding out cravings using Alan Marlatt's urge surfing technique. Includes animated wave visualization, box breathing guide (4-4-4-4), rotating mindfulness prompts, and configurable countdown timer (5-30 minutes). Based on Mindfulness-Based Relapse Prevention (MBRP) research.
- Time: ~15 minutes
- Questions answered: What is urge surfing? How to resist cravings. Craving timer. Mindfulness for addiction. How long do cravings last?

### Readiness to Change Assessment
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/readiness-to-change
- Description: Original educational assessment based on Prochaska & DiClemente's Transtheoretical Model (Stages of Change). 15 Likert-scale statements identify which stage of change you are in: Precontemplation, Contemplation, Preparation, Action, or Maintenance. Results include stage-specific next steps, recommended tools, and score breakdown across all five stages.
- Time: ~5 minutes
- Questions answered: What stage of change am I in? Am I ready to quit? Stages of change assessment. Readiness to change quiz. Motivation to change.

### Trigger Identification Worksheet
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/trigger-identification-worksheet
- Description: Interactive worksheet for identifying personal addiction triggers across six categories: People, Places, Emotional, Situational, Time-Based, and Sensory. Common triggers as checkboxes plus custom entries. Generates a personalized trigger profile with category-specific coping strategy suggestions. Printable output.
- Time: ~8 minutes
- Questions answered: What are my addiction triggers? Relapse triggers list. How to identify triggers. Addiction trigger categories. Coping strategies for triggers.

### Coping Skills Randomizer
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/coping-skills-randomizer
- Description: Random healthy coping skill generator with 51 evidence-based skills across 6 categories: Physical, Social, Creative, Mindfulness, Cognitive, and Sensory. Each skill includes step-by-step instructions. Category filtering, favorites, and full browse mode. Designed for use during cravings or difficult moments.
- Time: ~1 minute
- Questions answered: What to do instead of using? Coping skills for addiction. Healthy coping strategies. Random coping skill generator. Recovery coping tools.

### Daily Recovery Check-In
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/daily-recovery-check-in
- Description: Quick daily wellness check for people in recovery. Track mood, craving level, sleep quality, stress, connection to others, and physical health on 1-10 scales. Includes gratitude and goal entries. Saves to browser localStorage for 7-day and 30-day trend tracking. Streak counter for consecutive days checked in. Overall wellness score with color-coded bars.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Questions answered: Daily recovery check in. Sobriety check in. Recovery journal online. Daily mood tracker for recovery. How am I doing in recovery?

### Family Impact Assessment
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/family-impact-assessment
- Description: Original educational assessment for family members and loved ones concerned about someone's substance use. 18 Likert-scale questions across 7 domains: behavior changes, financial impact, relationship impact, impact on children/dependents, emotional toll, safety concerns, and enabling behaviors. Scores 0-72 across minimal, mild, moderate, and significant impact ranges. Results include validation, psychoeducation about addiction as a disease, domain-specific guidance, next steps (Al-Anon, Nar-Anon, family therapy), self-care recommendations, and safety alerts.
- Time: ~5 minutes
- Source: Original educational tool. Not a validated clinical instrument.
- Questions answered: Is my family member an addict quiz? How does addiction affect families? Am I enabling an addict? Family impact of addiction assessment. Concerned about someone's drinking.

### WHO ASSIST Substance Use Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/who-assist-substance-screening
- Description: WHO ASSIST v3.1 (Alcohol, Smoking and Substance Involvement Screening Test). Covers 10 substance categories: tobacco, alcohol, cannabis, cocaine, amphetamine-type stimulants, inhalants, sedatives, hallucinogens, opioids, and other. Stepped wizard interface asks lifetime use, then per-substance follow-up questions about frequency, cravings, problems, failed obligations, concern from others, and attempts to cut down. Substance-Specific Involvement Score (0-39) with three risk levels: low (no intervention), moderate (brief intervention), high (intensive treatment). Alcohol has different cutoffs (0-10 low) vs other substances (0-3 low). Includes Q8 injection drug use risk flag.
- Time: ~5-10 minutes
- Source: Developed by the World Health Organization. Validated across 8 countries. Public domain.
- Questions answered: WHO ASSIST screening. Substance use risk assessment. Drug and alcohol screening test. What is the ASSIST? ASSIST scoring. Substance involvement score.

### CRAFFT 2.1+N Adolescent Substance Screening
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/crafft-substance-screening
- Description: CRAFFT 2.1+N validated substance use screening for adolescents ages 12-21. Part A asks 4 yes/no questions about past-12-month use of alcohol, cannabis, other substances, and nicotine (+N). Part B asks 6 CRAFFT questions (Car, Relax, Alone, Forget, Friends/Family, Trouble) — or just the Car question if no substance use reported. Score 0-6, cutoff of 2+ is a positive screen. Age-appropriate language and design. Includes youth-specific crisis resources (Crisis Text Line, Teen Line).
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Source: Developed by Dr. John R. Knight et al. at Boston Children's Hospital/Harvard Medical School. Recommended by the American Academy of Pediatrics.
- Questions answered: CRAFFT screening for teens. Teen drug and alcohol screening. Adolescent substance use test. What is the CRAFFT? CRAFFT score meaning.

### ACE Questionnaire — Adverse Childhood Experiences
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/ace-questionnaire
- Description: 10-item Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACE) Questionnaire based on the CDC-Kaiser Permanente ACE Study (Felitti & Anda, 1995-1997). Yes/no questions about childhood adversity before age 18 across three categories: abuse (emotional, physical, sexual), neglect (emotional, physical), and household dysfunction (domestic violence, substance abuse, mental illness, parental separation, incarcerated household member). Score 0-10. Includes content warning gate, prominent resilience framing ("Your Score Is Not Your Destiny"), CDC-identified protective factors, and trauma-specific crisis resources (RAINN, Childhelp, SAMHSA, 988).
- Time: ~2-3 minutes
- Source: CDC-Kaiser Permanente Adverse Childhood Experiences Study (Felitti et al., 1998). Public domain.
- Questions answered: What is my ACE score? ACE questionnaire online. Adverse childhood experiences test. What does an ACE score mean? ACE score and health risks. Childhood trauma screening. Is a high ACE score a diagnosis?

### SPIN Social Anxiety Test
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/spin-social-anxiety-test
- Description: 17-item Social Phobia Inventory (SPIN) developed by Connor, Davidson, et al. (2000) at Duke University. Measures social anxiety across three subscales: Fear (6 items, 0-24), Avoidance (7 items, 0-28), and Physiological (4 items, 0-16). Total score 0-68. Five severity levels: 0-20 none/mild, 21-30 moderate, 31-40 severe, 41-50 very severe, 51-68 extremely severe. Clinical cutoff of 19+ for social anxiety disorder.
- Time: ~3 minutes
- Source: Developed by Connor, Davidson, Churchill, Sherwood, Foa, and Weisler (2000) at Duke University Medical Center.
- Questions answered: Do I have social anxiety? Social phobia test. SPIN social anxiety screening. What does my SPIN score mean? Social anxiety disorder test. Fear of social situations.

### Box Breathing Exercise
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/box-breathing-exercise
- Description: Free visual guided box breathing tool with animated square. Supports 3-3-3-3 (beginner), 4-4-4-4 (standard), 5-5-5-5 (advanced), and custom timing patterns. Session lengths of 2, 5, or 10 minutes. Includes cycle counter, optional ambient sound via Web Audio API, and calming visual animation synced to breathing phases. Used for anxiety, stress management, focus, and recovery support.
- Time: ~2-10 minutes
- Questions answered: What is box breathing? Box breathing exercise online. 4-4-4-4 breathing technique. Breathing exercise for anxiety. Square breathing. How to do box breathing.

### 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding Exercise
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/five-senses-grounding
- Description: Free guided 5-4-3-2-1 sensory grounding exercise for anxiety, panic attacks, and dissociation. Step-by-step tool walks through naming 5 things you see, 4 you touch, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, and 1 you taste. Text inputs for engagement, progress indicator, optional 10-second breathing pause between steps, and calming completion summary with all responses. Based on CBT/DBT distress tolerance skills.
- Time: ~3-5 minutes
- Questions answered: What is the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique? Grounding exercise for anxiety. Grounding technique for panic attacks. 5 senses grounding exercise. Sensory grounding for dissociation.

### Cognitive Distortion Identifier
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/cognitive-distortion-identifier
- Description: Free CBT-based educational tool for identifying cognitive distortions. User writes a negative thought, selects from 16 common thinking errors (all-or-nothing thinking, overgeneralization, catastrophizing, emotional reasoning, etc.) with descriptions and examples, then receives guided reframing prompts to write a balanced alternative thought. Based on the work of Aaron Beck and David Burns. Not AI-powered — user self-identifies distortions.
- Time: ~5 minutes
- Questions answered: What are cognitive distortions? Cognitive distortions list with examples. CBT thought record worksheet. How to reframe negative thoughts. Thinking errors CBT. Cognitive distortion identifier.

### Safety Plan
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/safety-plan
- Description: Free crisis safety planning tool based on the Stanley-Brown Safety Planning Intervention. 6-step guided plan: (1) warning signs, (2) internal coping strategies with suggestion chips, (3) people and places for distraction, (4) people to ask for help with phone numbers, (5) professionals and crisis contacts (988 Lifeline, Crisis Text Line, Veterans Crisis Line pre-filled), (6) making the environment safer. Generates printable plan. Auto-saves to browser localStorage. Crisis resources displayed prominently throughout. Designed with warmth and hope for people experiencing suicidal thoughts.
- Time: ~10-15 minutes
- Source: Based on Stanley & Brown Safety Planning Intervention (2012). Recommended by SPRC, VA, and Joint Commission.
- Questions answered: What is a safety plan? Safety plan template. Suicide safety plan. Crisis safety planning. Stanley-Brown safety plan. How to make a safety plan. 988 crisis lifeline.

### Work Stress & Burnout Check
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/work-stress-check
- Description: 12 original questions about work demands, control, support, and recovery.
- Time: ~3 minutes
- Questions answered: Am I burned out? Is my work stress normal? Work-life balance assessment.

### Mental Load Calculator
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/mental-load-calculator
- Description: See how planning, remembering, and organizing is distributed at home.
- Time: ~3 minutes
- Questions answered: Who carries the mental load? Household labor distribution.

### Sleep & Mood Reflection
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/sleep-and-mood-check
- Description: Explore how your sleep quality, habits, and mood affect each other.
- Time: ~2 minutes
- Questions answered: Is poor sleep affecting my mental health? Sleep-mood connection.

## Educational Guides

- PHQ-9 Guide: https://mindchecktools.com/blog/phq-9-guide
- GAD-7 Guide: https://mindchecktools.com/blog/gad-7-guide
- AUDIT Guide: https://mindchecktools.com/blog/audit-guide
- Work Stress vs Burnout: https://mindchecktools.com/blog/work-stress-vs-burnout
- Sleep and Mood: https://mindchecktools.com/blog/sleep-and-mood
- Seasonal Affective Disorder: https://mindchecktools.com/blog/seasonal-affective-disorder

## Crisis Resources
- URL: https://mindchecktools.com/crisis-resources
- 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (US): Call or text 988
- Crisis Text Line (US): Text HOME to 741741
- International: findahelpline.com

## Important Disclaimers
All tools are for educational and self-reflection purposes only. They are NOT a diagnosis, medical advice, or treatment recommendation. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional for mental health concerns. All responses are processed entirely in the browser and are never stored or transmitted.

## Technical Details
- Framework: Next.js (static export compatible)
- Hosting: Vercel
- Privacy: Zero server-side data collection, no accounts, all scoring is client-side JavaScript
- Ads: Google AdSense (ca-pub-7171402107622932) — ads never access screening responses
- Analytics: Google Analytics with Consent Mode v2

## Related Sites
- FiberTools: https://fibertools.app — Fiber arts calculators
- Creator Revenue Calculator: https://creatorrevenuecalculator.com — Creator monetization tools
- FlipMyCase: https://flipmycase.com — Text conversion utilities

## Contact
- Email: hello@mindchecktools.com
